THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
q0_ s&B
OFFICE OF THE GENERAL COUNSEL
.t\.t\
Jam€s E. Holst
CENERAL COTNSEL
August 1-6 , L99O
VIA EACAIUII,E & U.A. IIAIL
Scott Hallibran, Esq.
Acting Genera] counsel
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P, O. Box 807
sacramento, cal-ifornia 958 04
Re: Request for Fornal Advice Letter
Dear Mr. Hallibran:
I am seeking a formal advice letter regardinq participation by
Meredith Khachigian, a member of the Board of Regents of the
University of California, in a number of anticipated activities
of the Board and the site selection task force in connection with
choosing a site for a new canpus of the University of Catifornia,
as described below. I an Deputy General Counsel of The Regents
and an authorized by Regent Khachigian to make this request on
her behalf. I have spoken inforrnally about this rnatter to
Ms. Blanca Breeze of your staff.
The pertinent facts are as follows:
1. 
Meredith J. Khachigian is a member of the Board of Regents
of the University of California (the rrBoardtr).
2. 
In November 1988, the Board authorized University of
California President, David P. cardner, to begin the process
of planning for possible nee/ canpuses of the University.
3. 
In March 1989, President Gardner appointed a site selectj-on
task force (the rrtask force") made up of Regent Khachigian,
two other members of the Board, and six members of the
University faculty and staff. 
The task force is charged
with advising the President on the developnent and
application of criteria and methodofogy for choosing new
campus sites. 
The President considers the recomrnendations
of the task force in naking his recommendations to the
Board, which rnakes the final decisions.
-100 l,akesidc Drive, 7th Floor
Oakland. C:rlitornia 945 l2-.156-5
(.115) 987-9800 , 
FAX 987-9757


4.
scott Hallibran, Esq.
August L6, L99O
Page 2
In order to avoid any appearance of conflict of j-nterest in
the site sel,ection piocess, The Regents adopted special
financial, disclosure requirernents that before any action is
taken by the Board to narrow the l-ist of potential sites,
nembers of the Board disctose any economic or personal
interest they or nembers of their families may have related
to identified potential sites. 
The first 
such discl'osure
was due in connection with the July L9, 1-990 ' meeting of the
Board to consider reducing to eight the sites under
consi.deratj,on in the central region of the state. 
In early
Ju1y, in the process of naking this disclosure,
negent Khachigian was inforrned that a portion of one of the
eight sites was owned by !lr' Richard Gunner.
Regent Khachigiants husband, Kenneth, an attorney, had once
peiforrned lega1 services as described below for Mr. Gunner
on behalf of the Gunner Ranch (hereafter trcunnerrr).
In January L989, prior to Regent Khachigiants appointment to
the task iorce, and in connection with matters unrelated to
the University of cal-ifornia, Gunner contacted
Mr. Khachigian regarding advice on infrastructure
development in the vicinity of property he owned in Madera
county:-one parcel of which is now among the eight sites
referenced above.
on April 20, L989, Mr. Khachigian received a retainer fee of
more than $5oo fron Gunner. Thereafter, Mr. Khachigian
performed legal- services for Gunner rel-ated to their
discussion in January. These legal services were
discontinued as of JuIy 6, 1989, and no professional fees
were received by Mr. Khachigian subsequent to April 20.
Hov/ever, Mr. Khachigian did receive reimbursement for direct
out-of-pocket expenses in the sum of $523.88 on June 7,
1989, and in the surn of 5263.92 on August 7, L989. The
August 7 payment was the final reirnbursement of
ltr. Khachigian by cunner. Regent Khachigian has a community
property interest of less than $250 in the August 7 payment
and of more than $250 in the June 7 payment as well as the
Aprif 20 fee. No income of any kind has been received by
Mr. Khachigian from cunner during the Last L2 months.
Neither Mr. Khachigj-an nor Regent Khachigian has engaged in
any other contracts or business transactions with cunner.
At all times during the task forcets del-iberations and until
JuIy Ll-, 1990, the sites being considered by the task force
r{rere presented to its members by code designations. So, for
example, the site which includes the cunner property vras
never referred to by any of its owners' names, but as MD 1.
At the June 8, 1990, task force rneeting--at which sone 20
6.
7.
I


Scott Hallibran, Esq.
August 16, 1990
Page 3
sites were brought in by the University staff srith
recommendations to reduce the sel,ection to seven--the final
sites were still 
referenced by code designations. on
June 8, the task force received and accepted the
reconmendations of its staff.
8. 
On June 2!, L99o, President Gardner wrote to all the menbers
of the Board, enclosing a list of counties in which the
sites were located in order that further inforrnation could
be provided to rnembers who believed these counties might
potentially require further disclosure. Regent and
Mr. Khachigian were out of the state on vacation from July L
to July 8, 1990. Upon returning, the two of thern reviewed
President cardnerrs communication, and on July 11 replied by
facsinile transmission that three counties would requj-re
further inforrnation--Tu1are, Fresno and Madera. The
Khachigians have numerous relatives in Tulare and Fresno
Counties, and Mr. Khachigian had previous (though no active)
clients in all three counties.
9. 
On July 11, 1990, Regent Khachigian nade specific inquiries
to Unj.versity personnel about the sites and v/as j,nformed
that Mr. Gunner owned property in the site previously
ref erred to as MD 1. That !'ras the f irst time
Regent Khachigian became al^rare that a site under
consideration by the task force and the Board included real
property owned by her husbandrs former client.
10. Due to a previous commitment, Regent Khachigian did not
attend the July \9, L990, meeting of the Board at which the
Board voted to further consider the eight sites referred to
it, incl-uding the parcel owned or control-Ied by Gunner.
Thus, from the time Regent Khachigian ]earned of the
ownership by Gunner, she has not participated in any
decision relating to new site selection. Ho$/ever, I wish to
relate that on July 18, 1990, based on the foregoing facts,
this office rendered an opinion to Regent Khachigian that
she did not have a disqualifying financial interest and
could participate fu11y in site selection j-ssues.
11. Absent any disqualifying financial interest,
Regent Khachigian would anticipate participating in the
following activities of the task force and the Board:
a. 
In late Septenber or early October the members of the
task force plan to personally visit each of the eight
sites currently under consideration. t anticipate that
the sites wiII be the subject of informal discussion


scott Hallibran, Esq.
August 16, 1990
Page 4
b.
d.
among members of the task force, but no action $riII be
taken or reconnendation made at that tine.
Coincident with the site visits described above, the
task force will hold a series of public forums in the
areas of the potential sites. 
The task force plans to
listen to persons in affected communities; no action
will be taken or recommendation made by the task force
at that time.
on Novenber 9, :-990, the site selection task force wil-I
have a meeting io consider availabte information about
the eight sites under consideration. I anticipate that
each of the eight sites will then be subject to a vote
by Regent Khachigian and the members of the task force
as to whether it shouLd be recommended to
President Gardner. The task force will then forward
the results of its vote to President Gardner to use in
connection with his recommendation to the Board of
Regents at the Novenber meeting, when the Board plans
to reduce the eight sites to two or three. In voting
on v/hether to forward the sites for further
consideration by President Gardner, the task force will
be informed by a nurnber of reports from outside
consul,tants with respect to the criterj,a applicable to
narrowing the sites at this stage. The professional
staff of President cardner wil-I also offer its
recommendations to assist the site sefection task force
in its considerations.
At its November 1990 meeting the Board, including
Regent Khachigian, is scheduled to vote to narrow the
eight sites currently under consideration to two or
three for more intensive further consideration.
The current schedule calls for the Board, including
Regent Khachigian, to vote to choose a site for a new
campus at its November 1991 meeting after considering
environrnental irnpact report(s) and additionat detailed
data on the sites remaining under consideration.
**a**
In light of the above described financial interests of
Regent Khachigian (as conrnunity property interest in her
husbandrs incorne) and the fact that her duties as a nenber of the
task force and the Board may affect the site owned by Gunner, we
seek forrnal advice from the Comrnission, to wit: 
(a) may
Regent Khachigian continue to participate in trre aeiireiations


Scott Hal-libran, Esq.
August L6, 199 0
Page 5
and votes of the si-te seLection task force in connection with the
above-described reconmendations it nakes to the President of the
University who wi11, in turn, nake a recommendation to the Board;
and (b) nay Regent Khachigian participate in decisions by the
Board to narrow the list of sites and to finally choose the site?
could you please expedite your response in order for
Regent Khachigian to have guidance in tine for the late septenber
site tours and public forums?
Thank you very much for your assistance, and
if f can provide any additional inforrnqtion.
pJ.ease l-et me knour
Morr i son
Generaf counsel

M. J. Khachigian

sections.question: In light of the above described financial interests of Regent Khachigian (as community property interest in her husband's income) and the fact that her duties as a member of the task force and the Board may affect the site owned by Gunner, we seek formal advice from the Commission, to wit: (a) may Regent Khachigian continue to participate in the deliberations and votes of the site selection task force in connection with the above-described recommendations it makes to the President of the University who will, in turn, make a recommendation to the Board; and (b) may Regent Khachigian participate in decisions by the Board to narrow the list of sites and to finally choose the site?

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Regent Khachigian, who has a community property interest in her husband's past legal fees from a client (Gunner) who owns property in one of the sites under consideration for a new UC campus, may participate in task force and Board decisions regarding site selection.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from UC General Counsel to FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document contains detailed factual background and dual-part legal questions but no FPPC conclusion or advice. Document is dated August 16, 1990 (Document ID 90538).

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: In light of the above described financial interests of Regent Khachigian (as community property interest in her husband's income) and the fact that her duties as a member of the task force and the Board may affect the site owned by Gunner, we seek formal advice from the Commission, to wit: (a) may Regent Khachigian continue to participate in the deliberations and votes of the site selection task force in connection with the above-described recommendations it makes to the President of the University who will, in turn, make a recommendation to the Board; and (b) may Regent Khachigian participate in decisions by the Board to narrow the list of sites and to finally choose the site?

embedding.summary: The Regents of the University of California request a formal FPPC advice letter regarding whether Board member Meredith Khachigian may participate in site selection decisions for a new campus when her husband previously provided legal services to a person who owns property on one of the candidate sites.
